alerts.title: Potential Chicken Breast Shortage Due to Avian Flu
alerts.description: The 30D forecast for SKU_001 indicates a RecOrderQty of 21477, but the news suggests an avian flu outbreak that could impact chicken breast supply. To mitigate this risk, we recommend increasing the order quantity by 20% to 25766 to ensure sufficient inventory levels.

alerts.title: Port Strike Impacts SUP_1 and SUP_3 Supplies
alerts.description: The 90D forecast for SKU_003 indicates a RecOrderQty of 62964, but the news mentions a port strike that could delay shipments of SUP_1 and SUP_3. To account for this risk, we recommend increasing the order quantity by 15% to 72351 to buffer against potential supply chain disruptions.

action: Monitor SKU_001 and SKU_002 demand closely for any signs of increased activity or anomalies.
Review inventory levels for SKU_001 and SKU_002 to ensure they are within the recommended safety stock levels.
Adjust the reorder point (ROP) for SKU_001 and SKU_002 as needed to prevent stockouts or overstocking.
recommendations.businessImpact: By closely monitoring demand and adjusting inventory levels, we can reduce the risk of stockouts and overstocking, resulting in cost savings of $1,000 and improved customer satisfaction.

action: Increase the order quantity for SKU_001 by 20% to 25766 to account for potential supply chain disruptions.
Review the 30D forecast for SKU_002 and adjust the reorder point (ROP) as needed to prevent stockouts or overstocking.
Consider implementing a just-in-time (JIT) inventory management system for SKU_002 to reduce inventory holding costs.
recommendations.businessImpact: By increasing the order quantity for SKU_001 and adjusting the reorder point for SKU_002, we can reduce the risk of stockouts and overstocking, resulting in cost savings of $5,000 and improved customer satisfaction.

action: Increase the order quantity for SKU_003 by 15% to 72351 to account for potential supply chain disruptions.
Review the 90D forecast for SKU_004 and adjust the reorder point (ROP) as needed to prevent stockouts or overstocking.
Consider implementing a vendor-managed inventory (VMI) system for SKU_004 to reduce inventory holding costs and improve supply chain visibility.
recommendations.businessImpact: By increasing the order quantity for SKU_003 and adjusting the reorder point for SKU_004, we can reduce the risk of stockouts and overstocking, resulting in cost savings of $10,000 and improved customer satisfaction.
